name: slow-grower-dividend
query: Research GASN (Granite State Natural Gas) using the given facts. I want something safe that pays me.

given_facts.business: Regulated gas utility serving northern New England; demand grows roughly with population.
given_facts.dividend_note: paid and raised for 31 consecutive years; payout ~73% of earnings
given_facts.debt_note: normal regulated-utility leverage; investment grade; no maturity wall before 2030

expected_behavior: Classifies GASN as a Slow grower (mature utility, ~3% growth, generous dividend) and sets the expectation: you own this for the dividend, not for capital appreciation - and says so plainly.
Computes P/E ~14.6 (38 / 2.60) and dividend yield ~5% (1.90 / 38) with compute_valuation.py; notes plain PEG is nearly meaningless at 3% growth and uses the dividend-adjusted view instead.
Judges the slow-grower checklist questions: has the dividend always been paid/raised (31 years - yes), and is the payout ratio safe (~73% - acceptable for a regulated utility)?
Does NOT promise a tenbagger or upgrade the category because the yield is attractive; a slow grower is ballast, not upside.
Checks the balance sheet honestly: utility-normal heavy debt, investment grade, no near-term wall - and does not present cash minus debt as if it were a Lynch net-cash cushion for an industrial.
Includes a bear case: rate-case risk (regulator denies increases), gas-to-electric transition risk over decades, payout creep if earnings stall.
Sets category-correct sell triggers: sell if the dividend is cut/frozen or payout becomes unsustainable, or when a better-category idea needs the capital - the ch. 17 slow-grower rules.
Every figure cites a source + as-of date.
